question.text: Capital gains on the transfer of properties held by a REIT are taxed at which level?

answer.text: At the REIT level, NOT passed through to unit-holders
At the unit-holder level through pass-through mechanism
At both the REIT level and unit-holder level
Exempt at both REIT level and unit-holder level

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Capital gains on the transfer of properties by the REIT are taxed at the REIT level itself. Unlike rental income, interest, and dividend which enjoy pass-through status, capital gains from property disposal do NOT pass through to unit-holders. **The Trap:** "At the unit-holder level through pass-through" is tempting because most REIT income types (rental, interest, dividend) DO have pass-through status. Students who remember the pass-through principle may incorrectly apply it to capital gains on property transfer as well. **Cross-Exam Context:** This distinction between pass-through and non-pass-through income types within the same vehicle is a favorite testing area in NISM Series X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Capital gains on property transfer at the REIT level can impact NAV but not directly hit unit-holders' tax liability. However, capital gains on transfer of REIT units by investors ARE taxable for unit-holders -- don't confuse the two.
